result.summary: The page is mostly usable, but several elements appear clipped or overflowing horizontally, especially on the right side. There are also some fixed-position and carousel controls that visually overlap or obscure content.

issues.description: Multiple horizontal sections overflow the viewport. The top category tab row cuts off the 'Hot Games' tab, and the game cards on the right side are partially clipped.
issues.suggestion: Constrain content to the viewport width or make these areas clearly scrollable carousels with proper padding, snap behavior, and visible scroll affordance.

issues.description: The game grid/card layout appears too wide for the mobile viewport, showing partial third-column cards instead of a clean responsive layout.
issues.suggestion: Use a mobile-optimized layout, such as two columns with consistent gutters, or convert the rows into intentional horizontal carousels with evenly sized cards and edge spacing.

issues.description: The bottom navigation bar overlays the lower game content, making the bottom row look partially hidden behind the fixed nav.
issues.suggestion: Add sufficient bottom padding or safe-area spacing to the scrollable content so cards are not obscured by the fixed navigation.

issues.description: The hero carousel arrows are partially clipped at the page edges and overlap the banner artwork, which looks visually rough.
issues.suggestion: Inset the carousel controls slightly from the edges and ensure they remain fully visible with adequate touch target spacing.

issues.description: The top header branding area looks crowded, with multiple logos and the state label compressed into a small horizontal space.
issues.suggestion: Simplify the header on mobile, reduce logo count/size, or stack secondary branding so the header feels less cramped.
